name: Cocoa Chiffon Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon cocoa plus extra cocoa for preparing pan
5 large egg whites, at room temperature
2 tablespoons confectioner's sugar, sifted plus extra for dusting cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees F.
2. Brush the inside of a 9-inch Bundt or springform pan with oil and sprinkle with a little cocoa. Tap out excess.
3. In a large bowl, sift together flour, 1/3 cup plus 1 tablespoon sifted Dutch-process cocoa, sugar, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients. Add oil, 1/2 cup warm water (plus 2 tablespoons), vanilla, and egg yolk. Mix gently; do not overmix.
5. In another large bowl, beat egg whites with cream of tartar until foamy. Gradually add 2 tablespoons confectioner's sugar and continue beating until stiff but not dry. Gently fold the egg white mixture into the batter using a spatula at low speed.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top with a rubber spatula. Tap the pan once to release air bubbles.
7. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean and the top feels springy (the top may be cracked).
8. Allow the cake to cool upright on a wire rack for 10 minutes. Loosen the sides and center with a knife, invert onto the rack, and let it cool completely before serving. Dust lightly with confectioner's sugar.
Serves 16.
